data.narrative:
Skydio, a Series E autonomous systems company in Defense & Security, was last valued at $2.2B post-money after raising $340M+ cumulatively. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies across the sector: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Flock Safety, Anduril Industries, and Shield AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $2.2B last post-money as a base and applies a 1.4x multiplier typical for Series E advancement, yielding $3.1B. Peer-median analysis references 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Saronic at $9.2B (Series D), Flock Safety at $7.5B (Series E), Shield AI at $5.6B (Series F), Chaos Industries at $4.5B (Series D), and Epirus at $1.4B (Series D). The median valuation across this set is $5.6B, with a range of $1.4B to $9.2B.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $2.6B to $6.4B, with a median of $4.3B. The current $2.2B valuation sits below the stage-progression and significantly below peer median, suggesting either a discount relative to comparables or conservative pricing in the last round. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, customer concentration, and whether peer valuations reflect recent mark-downs in defense-tech sectors.
